description: Curated page-owned refinements that override generic or degraded source fields without changing the frozen curriculum objective.

learning.student_instruction: Say, ‘My name is ___.’ Trace your name. Then write it once.
learning.expected_response: Child says own name, traces the adult-written model, and makes one supported copy attempt.

teacher.model: Write the child’s name on the trace guide. Model the sentence once and guide the first movement.
teacher.question: What is your name?

guidance.parent_extension: Say family names together and clap the syllables.

illustration.scene: One happy Nursery child pointing to self while looking into a simple standing mirror. Keep the lower writing area completely clear.
illustration.focal_point: The child and mirror support self-recognition; all name-writing guides are built separately by the page composer.
protected_response_zones: entire deterministic trace-and-copy area below the illustration

learning.student_instruction: Name red, yellow, and blue. Use the three colours to create your own picture.
learning.expected_response: Child names the three primary colours and uses red, yellow, and blue in a simple artwork.

teacher.model: Name the three colours and show one small example. Leave the main space for the child.
teacher.question: Which colour will you use first?

guidance.parent_extension: Find one red, one yellow, and one blue object at home.

illustration.scene: Three large open paint pots—red, yellow, and blue—beside one simple child-like model picture made only with those colours.
illustration.focal_point: The three primary colours are unmistakable and the sample remains simple, while the large child artwork area stays empty and unobstructed.
protected_response_zones: large deterministic creative-response area below the illustration
